records.ety: [OE. trone, F. trne, L. thronus, Gr. <?/; cf. <?/ a bench, <?/ a footstool, <?/ to set one's self, to sit, Skr. dharaa supporting, dh to hold fast, carry, and E. firm, a.]

defs: A chair of state, commonly a royal seat, but sometimes the seat of a prince, bishop, or other high dignitary.
quotes: The noble king is set up in his throne.
High on a throne of royal state.

defs: Hence, sovereign power and dignity; also, the one who occupies a throne, or is invested with sovereign authority; an exalted or dignified personage.
quotes: Only in the throne will I be greater than thou.
To mold a mighty state's decrees, And shape the whisper of the throne.

defs: A high order of angels in the celestial hierarchy; -- a meaning given by the schoolmen.
quotes: Great Sire! whom thrones celestial ceaseless sing.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Throned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Throning.]

defs: To place on a royal seat; to enthrone.
To place in an elevated position; to give sovereignty or dominion to; to exalt.
quotes: True image of the Father, whether throned In the bosom of bliss, and light of light.

defs: To be in, or sit upon, a throne; to be placed as if upon a throne.
